text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a software development practice that helps teams work together more efficiently. In a CI workflow, developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared main branch. Every time new code is added, automated builds are triggered, meaning the software is compiled and assembled without manual effort. During this process, CI also runs tests to check if the latest changes break any existing features. By catching issues early in the development cycle, developers can fix problems quickly, saving time and reducing errors. These steps are organized into CI pipelines, which are automated sequences that process the code from start to finish.
